MINI MINUTES FOR JUL-02-25 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 501

REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANT EQUITY RESIDENTIAL MANAGEMENT LLC'S MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS GRANTED, IN PART, AND DENIED, IN PART. PARAGRAPH 24 IS STRICKEN WITH LEAVE TO AMEND TO ALLEGE PUNITIVE DAMAGES AS TO THE CORPORATE DEFENDANT WITH SUFFICIENT PARTICULARITY. THERE ARE INSUFFICIENT FACTS SUPPORTING DEFENDANT'S ADVANCE KNOWLEDGE, AUTHORIZATION, OR RATIFICATION WITH RESPECT TO THE IDENTIFIED INDIVIDUALS' RESPONSIBILITIES AND CONDUCT. THE COURT ALSO NOTES THAT ALLEGATIONS NUMBERS (2) AND (3) WITHIN PARAGRAPH 24 DO NOT IDENTIFY INDIVIDUALS; THE GRAMMATIC STRUCTURE OF THIS PARAGRAPH SUGGESTS THAT PLAINTIFF IS ALLEGING A CORPORATE DEFENDANT AUTHORIZED CONDUCT OR WAS PERSONALLY GUILTY. ALL OTHER REQUESTS TO STRIKE ARE DENIED; THE FAC ALLEGES DEFENDANT "INTENTIONALLY CONCEALED" HABITABILITY PROBLEMS FROM PLAINTIFF PRIOR TO SIGNING THE LEASE, THAT PLAINTIFF NOTIFIED DEFENDANT ABOUT THE RODENTS, AND THAT DEFENDANT IGNORED REQUESTS FOR REPAIRS. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER IN ACCORDANCE WITH CRC RULE 3.1312(B). JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES; CLERK: KENNETH B. HUNT; NOT REPORTED. (D501)

LAW & MOTION, DEPT. 302, AS TO THE OCT-03-2024 HEARING RE: DEFENDANT GEARY COURTYARD ASSOCIATES' MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT, THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. TRANSFERRED TO DEPARTMENT 501 TO BE HEARD ON OCTOBER 18, 2024, AT 9:30 A.M. THAT DEPARTMENT HANDLES ALL LANDLORD-TENANT DISPUTES PER SF LOCAL RULE 8.10A1. BOTH PARTIES TO COMPLY WITH SFLR 2.7B AND DELIVER COURTESY COPIES OF THEIR RESPECTIVE FILINGS IN SUPPORT OF AND IN OPPOSITION TO THE MOTION TO DEPARTMENT 501 WITH A COVER LETTER REFLECTING THE NEW HEARING DATE AND DEPARTMENT NO LATER THAN OCTOBER 17, 2024. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER JR.; CLERK: S. KANE; NOT REPORTED. (302/RBU)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jul-09-2025 Continued To Oct-22-25 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION TO STRIKE Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-02-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT EQUITY RESIDENTIAL MANAGEMENT LLC'S MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is GRANTED, in part, and DENIED, in part. Paragraph 24 is stricken with leave to amend to allege punitive damages as to the corporate Defendant with sufficient particularity. There are insufficient facts supporting Defendant's advance knowledge, authorization, or ratification with respect to the identified individuals' responsibilities and conduct. The Court also notes that allegations numbers (2) and (3) within paragraph 24 do not identify individuals; the grammatic structure of this paragraph suggests that Plaintiff is alleging a corporate Defendant authorized conduct or was personally guilty.
All other requests to strike are denied; the FAC alleges Defendant "intentionally concealed" habitability problems from Plaintiff prior to signing the lease, that Plaintiff notified Defendant about the rodents, and that Defendant ignored requests for repairs.

Prevailing party to submit a proposed order in accordance with CRC Rule 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not Reported. (D501)
